text: Level Nine is running 20% off tunes through Nov 30 if you want to add mine to the drop-off.
Nice — I'll take yours along with Ethan's on Saturday morning.
Sian frame is 60% done. Ethan lost steam on the sequential differential — blocking off Saturday afternoon to push through it together.
Closing — both kids' photos and paperwork mailed to State Dept. Tracking confirmation received.
REI has the Subaru-fit chains in stock at Sugar House. Grab the auto-tension model, not the old cable kind.
Got them yesterday. Also grabbed a spare tow strap and a bag of kitty litter for traction.
12 uphill days is aggressive with custody-week constraints. 9-10 is more honest — don't burn yourself out chasing the number.
Deciding between overhead ceiling racks and wall shelves for ski and bike storage. Leaning overhead so the floor stays clear for the trash bins.
Overhead. Get the Fleximounts 4x8 platform — I put two up in my garage last spring and they've held up great.
Adding Ms. Whittaker's notes on Ethan's writing progress. He's one grade level behind — she's recommending 20 minutes of daily reading logs.
I have GPX traces from my 2025 attempt on the Highline. Sharing tomorrow morning after I pull them off my Garmin.
Creating a follow-up chore in db-tasks to reserve car shuttle from Highline Trailhead. Christi Shuttle service was recommended on the local FB group.
Lily picked plant-growth-under-different-light-colors for her science fair project. Buying LEDs and bean seeds this weekend at Home Depot.
I can take her to the Sugar House Home Depot on Thursday to grab the seedling trays. Off-custody week but happy to help.
Beds mulched, drip lines drained and blown out. Still need to wrap the fig tree in burlap before the first hard freeze this week.
Finished 'Range' by David Epstein. Halfway through 'How to Read a Book' by Adler — the analytical reading chapter is dense but worth it. 4 more books to hit the 6-nonfiction target.
Dr. Gibbs cleared Lily on iron levels. Continue the supplement 6 more weeks then re-check labs in mid-January.
Bear Lake State Park cabins are impossible to book in Feb. Try VRBO in Garden City instead — solid ones that sleep 6-8 on the north shore.
Booked a 3BR in Garden City for Feb 13-16. Confirmation saved to Google Drive. Kevin's boys coming with us so it's 4 kids and 2 adults.
November spend was $340 over on groceries and gear. Reallocating from personal-cash bucket to cover the overage through end of Q4.
